zotero.abstractNote: Algorithmic decision-making (ADM) is increasingly used in healthcare. While its potential to improve clinical outcomes is widely acknowledged, it also raises significant ethical concerns. In this paper, we provide a systematic overview of the ethical challenges associated with ADM in healthcare. We argue that these challenges can be structured along three dimensions: (1) the epistemic dimension, which concerns the quality and reliability of the data and the algorithms; (2) the normative dimension, which concerns the values and principles that should guide the development and deployment of ADM; and (3) the social dimension, which concerns the impact of ADM on the doctor-patient relationship and the broader healthcare system. We conclude by outlining a research agenda for the ethical development and implementation of ADM in healthcare.
